116	def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
117	    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False
118	
119	    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
120	    sentencepiece_model = False
121	    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
122	    created_folder = False
123	    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
124	        created_folder = True
125	        os.makedirs(file_location)
126	    pass
127	    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
128	    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
129	        sentencepiece_model = True
130	    pass
131	    if created_folder:
132	        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
133	    return sentencepiece_model

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

204	@torch.inference_mode
205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)

465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass
175

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out
190	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

120	def _fast_prepare_inputs_for_generation(self, input_ids, attention_mask=None, **kwargs,):
121	    past_key_values = kwargs.get("past_key_values", None)
122	    if past_key_values is not None:
123	        # Check for uninitialized DynamicCache

125	            past_key_values = None
126	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
127	        # New since 4.56
128	        elif hasattr(past_key_values, "get_seq_length") and past_key_values.get_seq_length() == 0:
129	            past_key_values = None
130	            kwargs["past_key_values"] = None
131	        else:
132	            bs, cache_length = input_ids.shape
133	            input_ids = input_ids[:,[-1]]
134	
135	            # Get to the base model
136	            base_model = self
137	            if hasattr(base_model, 'base_model_prefix'):
138	                base_model = getattr(base_model, base_model.base_model_prefix)
139	
140	            if hasattr(base_model, "_prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position"):
141	                def needs_device_kw(fn) -> bool:
142	                    try:
143	                        sig = inspect.signature(inspect.unwrap(fn))
144	                        return "device" in sig.parameters
145	                    except:
146	                        # transformers <= 4.51.3 includes device arg but > 4.51.3 does not
147	                        return transformers_version < Version("4.52.0")
148	
149	                kwargs = {
150	                    "sequence_length": 1,
151	                    "target_length": cache_length,
152	                    "dtype": self.dtype,
153	                    "cache_position": torch.arange(cache_length, cache_length+1, device=input_ids.device),
154	                    "batch_size": bs,
155	                    "config": self.config,
156	                    "past_key_values": past_key_values,
157	                }
158	                try:
159	                    if needs_device_kw(base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position):
160	                        kwargs["device"] = input_ids.device
161	                except:
162	                    print(f"Unsloth: Could not inspect signature of {base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position}")
163	
164	                attention_mask = base_model._prepare_4d_causal_attention_mask_with_cache_position(

174	                        "writing code, see Llama for an example implementation. If you're a user, please report this "
175	                        "issue on GitHub."
176	                    )

2571	    import types
2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

2574	    # And now re add our saving methods!

2581	    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")

2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """

2591	    del arguments["self"]

2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]

2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:

2602	        if commit_message is not None:

2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:

2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]

2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619

2626	        )
2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

228	                revision = revision,
229	                trust_remote_code = trust_remote_code,
230	            )
231	            is_peft = True
232	        except Exception as error:
233	            peft_error = str(error)
234	            is_peft = False
235	        pass
236	
237	        # Old transformers versions check
238	        both_exist = (is_model and is_peft) and not SUPPORTS_LLAMA32
239	
240	        # Error out if both LoRA and normal model config exists.
241	        if both_exist:
242	            raise RuntimeError(
243	                "Unsloth: Your repo has a LoRA adapter and a base model.\n"\
244	                "You have 2 files `config.json` and `adapter_config.json`.\n"\
245	                "We must only allow one config file.\n"\
246	                "Please separate the LoRA and base models to 2 repos."
247	            )
248	        model_types = get_transformers_model_type(
249	            peft_config if peft_config is not None else model_config
250	        )
251	        if len(model_types) == 1:
252	            model_type = model_types[0]
253	        else:
254	            # Leave as tuple if more than one arch
255	            model_type = model_types
256	
257	        # New transformers need to check manually.
258	        if SUPPORTS_LLAMA32:
259	            # Check if folder exists locally
260	            if os.path.isdir(model_name):
261	                exist_adapter_config = os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_name, "adapter_config.json"))
262	                exist_config         = os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_name, "config.json"))
263	                both_exist = exist_adapter_config and exist_config
264	            else:
265	                # Because HfFileSystem assumes linux paths, we need to set the path with forward slashes, even on Windows.
266	                files = HfFileSystem(token = token).glob(f"{model_name}/*.json")
267	                files = list(os.path.split(x)[-1] for x in files)
268	                if sum(x == "adapter_config.json" or x == "config.json" for x in files) >= 2:
269	                    both_exist = True
270	                pass
271	            pass
272	        pass
273	
274	        if not is_model and not is_peft:
275	            error = autoconfig_error if autoconfig_error is not None else peft_error
276	            # Old transformers version
277	            if "rope_scaling" in error.lower() and not SUPPORTS_LLAMA31:
278	                raise ImportError(
279	                    f"Unsloth: Your transformers version of {transformers_version} does not support new RoPE scaling methods.\n"\
280	                    f"This includes Llama 3.1. The minimum required version is 4.43.2\n"\
281	                    f'Try `pip install --upgrade "transformers>=4.43.2"`\n'\
282	                    f"to obtain the latest transformers build, then restart this session."\
283	                )
284	            # Create a combined error message showing both failures
285	            combined_error = (
286	                "Unsloth: Failed to load model. Both AutoConfig and PeftConfig loading failed.\n\n"
287	                f"AutoConfig error: {autoconfig_error}\n\n"
288	                f"PeftConfig error: {peft_error}\n\n"
289	            )
290	            raise RuntimeError(combined_error)
291	        pass
292	
293	        # Get base model for PEFT:
294	        if is_peft:
295	            # Check base model again for PEFT
296	            model_name = peft_config.base_model_name_or_path
297	            if not use_exact_model_name:
298	                model_name = get_model_name(model_name, load_in_4bit)
299	            model_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(
300	                model_name,
301	                token = token,
302	                trust_remote_code = trust_remote_code,
303	            )
304	        pass
305	
306	        if not was_disabled: enable_progress_bars()
307	
308	        if model_type == "llama":
309	            scaling_type = None
310	            if getattr(model_config, "rope_scaling", None) is not None:
311	                scaling_type1 = model_config.rope_scaling.get("type", None)
312	                scaling_type2 = model_config.rope_scaling.get("rope_type", None)
313	                scaling_type = scaling_type1 if scaling_type1 is not None else scaling_type2
314	            pass
315	
316	            if scaling_type == "llama3" and not SUPPORTS_LLAMA31:
317	                raise ImportError(
318	                    f"Unsloth: Your transformers version of {transformers_version} does not support Llama 3.1.\n"\
319	                    f"The minimum required version is 4.43.2\n"\
320	                    f'Try `pip install --upgrade "transformers>=4.43.2"`\n'\
321	                    f"to obtain the latest transformers build, then restart this session."\
322	                )
323	
324	            dispatch_model = FastLlamaModel
325	
326	        elif model_type == "mistral": dispatch_model = FastMistralModel
327	        elif model_type == "gemma":

331	                    f"The minimum required version is 4.38.\n"\
332	                    f'Try `pip install --upgrade "transformers>=4.38"`\n'\
333	                    f"to obtain the latest transformers build, then restart this session."\
334	                )
335	            dispatch_model = FastGemmaModel
336	        elif model_type == "gemma2":

338	                raise ImportError(

340	                    f"The minimum required version is 4.42.3.\n"\
341	                    f'Try `pip install --upgrade "transformers>=4.42.3"`\n'\
342	                    f"to obtain the latest transformers build, then restart this session."\
343	                )

348	                    "To install flash-attn, do the below:\n"\

350	                )
351	            elif HAS_FLASH_ATTENTION and not HAS_FLASH_ATTENTION_SOFTCAPPING:

Excellent! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report of the unsloth codebase structure:

## Unsloth Codebase Structure Report

### Overall Directory Layout

The unsloth codebase is organized as a Python package with the following top-level structure:

**Root Level Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/__init__.py` - Main package initialization
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/save.py` - **Core model saving/export functionality** (105KB)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/chat_templates.py` - Chat template utilities
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` - Tokenizer utility functions
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/trainer.py` - Trainer patches
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/import_fixes.py` - Import-time patching
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/_auto_install.py` - Auto-installation utilities

### Key Subdirectories

#### 1. **models/** - Model implementations and loaders (20 files)
   - `__init__.py` - Exports FastLlamaModel, FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, etc.
   - `loader.py` - Main model loading functions (FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel)
   - `loader_utils.py` - Utility functions for loading
   - `mapper.py` - Model mapping functionality
   - `_utils.py` - Utility functions (is_bfloat16_supported, __version__)
   
   **Model-specific implementations:**
   - `llama.py` (131KB) - Llama model optimizations
   - `llama4.py` - Llama 4 model
   - `mistral.py` - Mistral model
   - `qwen2.py` - Qwen 2 model
   - `qwen3.py` - Qwen 3 model
   - `qwen3_moe.py` - Qwen 3 MoE model
   - `gemma.py` - Gemma model
   - `gemma2.py` - Gemma 2 model
   - `granite.py` - Granite model
   - `falcon_h1.py` - Falcon H1 model
   - `cohere.py` - Cohere model
   - `dpo.py` - DPO training patches
   - `rl.py` (47KB) - Reinforcement learning patches
   - `rl_replacements.py` (31KB) - RL model replacements
   - `vision.py` (47KB) - Vision model support

#### 2. **kernels/** - Optimized CUDA kernels (10 files)
   - `__init__.py` - Kernel exports
   - `fast_lora.py` - LoRA kernel optimizations
   - `cross_entropy_loss.py` - Fast CE loss kernel
   - `rope_embedding.py` - RoPE embedding kernel
   - `layernorm.py` - Layer norm kernel
   - `rms_layernorm.py` - RMS layer norm kernel
   - `geglu.py` - GEGLU activation kernel
   - `swiglu.py` - SwiGLU activation kernel
   - `flex_attention.py` - Flex attention kernel
   - `utils.py` - Kernel utilities
   
   **moe/** subdirectory - Mixture of Experts support:
   - `__init__.py`
   - `grouped_gemm/` - Grouped GEMM kernels for MoE
   - `tests/` - MoE tests
   - `benchmark/` - MoE benchmarking utilities

#### 3. **registry/** - Model registry system (8 files)
   - `__init__.py` - Exports register_models(), search_models(), MODEL_REGISTRY
   - `registry.py` - Core ModelInfo and QuantType definitions
   - `REGISTRY.md` - Registry documentation
   - `_deepseek.py` - Deepseek model registration
   - `_gemma.py` - Gemma model registration
   - `_llama.py` - Llama model registration
   - `_mistral.py` - Mistral model registration
   - `_phi.py` - Phi model registration
   - `_qwen.py` - Qwen model registration

#### 4. **dataprep/** - Data preparation utilities (3 files)
   - `__init__.py` - Exports synthetic dataset functionality
   - `synthetic.py` - Synthetic dataset generation
   - `synthetic_configs.py` - Configuration for synthetic data

#### 5. **utils/** - Miscellaneous utilities (2 files)
   - `__init__.py` - Empty
   - `hf_hub.py` - Hugging Face hub utilities

### Save/Export Methods Location

All save and export functionality is **centralized in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/save.py`** (105KB file with 71 functions/classes)

**Key export/save functions in save.py:**

1. **unsloth_save_model()** (line 205) - Main saving function supporting:
   - `save_method` options: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
   - Optional push to HuggingFace Hub
   - Memory-efficient streaming saves

2. **save_to_gguf()** (line 952) - GGUF format conversion:
   - Quantization options: q8_0, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q4_k_s, q5_k_s, q3_k_l, q3_k_m, q3_k_s, etc.
   - Supports llama.cpp integration

3. **unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()** (line 1321) - Save merged model in HF format

4. **unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()** (line 1364) - Push merged model to Hub

5. **create_huggingface_repo()** (line 1456) - Create HF repo

6. **unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()** (line 1768) - Save model as GGUF

7. **unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()** (line 1951) - Push GGUF to Hub

8. **unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()** (line 2211) - Convert LoRA to GGML

9. **unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()** (line 2143) - Convert and push LoRA

10. **patch_saving_functions()** (line 2569) - Monkey-patches model saving methods

11. **Ollama integration:**
    - `create_ollama_modelfile()` (line 1618)
    - `create_ollama_model()` (line 1663)
    - `push_to_ollama()` (line 1735)

### Package Organization (__init__.py)

The top-level `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/__init__.py` (11KB):

1. **Initialization Flow:**
   - Fixes import ordering issues (warns if transformers/peft/trl imported first)
   - Sets CUDA environment variables for memory optimization
   - Validates PyTorch 2.0+ installation
   - Detects GPU type (CUDA, HIP, XPU)
   - Links CUDA libraries with ldconfig if needed
   - Verifies unsloth_zoo installation

2. **Exports (via `from .X import *`):**
   - `from .models import *` - All model classes and FastLanguageModel
   - `from .save import *` - All saving/export functions
   - `from .chat_templates import *` - Chat formatting utilities
   - `from .tokenizer_utils import *` - Tokenizer utilities
   - `from .trainer import *` - Trainer patches

3. **Key Imports:**
   - Patches TRL trainers for backward compatibility

### Test Structure

Test files are organized in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/tests/`:
- `saving/` - Save/export functionality tests
  - `language_models/` - Language model saving tests
  - `vision_models/` - Vision model saving tests
  - `text_to_speech_models/` - TTS model tests
  - `non_peft/` - Non-PEFT model tests
  - `gpt-oss-merge/` - GPT-OSS merge tests
- `qlora/` - QLoRA training tests
- `utils/` - Test utilities and benchmarks

### Summary Statistics
- **Total Python files:** 71
- **Main package files:** 7
- **Model implementations:** 20 files
- **Kernel optimizations:** 10 files (+MOE submodule)
- **Registry system:** 8 files
- **Data preparation:** 3 files

The codebase is well-organized with clear separation of concerns: models handle architecture-specific optimizations, kernels provide CUDA acceleration, the registry enables model discovery, and save.py centralizes all export/serialization logic.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the unsloth save/export system. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary Report: Unsloth Save/Export Source Files

I have thoroughly explored the unsloth codebase and identified all save/export related source files. Here are my findings:

### Primary Save/Export File

**File Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/save.py`

**Size:** 103 KB (2,678 lines)

**Key Exports (__all__):**
- `print_quantization_methods`
- `unsloth_save_model`
- `save_to_gguf`
- `patch_saving_functions`
- `create_huggingface_repo`

---

### Core Functions and Methods (by category)

#### 1. **Primary Model Saving Functions**

| Function | Lines | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None) or getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default",))

```

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/save.py`

Key pattern for merging LoRA weights:
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if quant_state is not None:
        W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
    
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    
    if A is not None:
        # Merge formula: W += s * (A.t() @ B.t())
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

Now let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Unsloth LoRA Implementation - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on a thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase, here is the detailed analysis of how LoRA adapters are implemented:

### 1. **LoRA Weight Storage and Access**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)

The LoRA weights are accessed through PEFT's dictionary-based structure:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # Access pattern:
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dictionary keyed by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                # Get .weight from Linear layer
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
```

**Key Points:**
- `lora_A` and `lora_B` are dictionaries keyed by adapter name (e.g., "default")
- Each entry is a Linear layer (from PEFT's Linear adapter)
- Actual weights accessed via `.weight` attribute
- Returns 5-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`

**Related Functions:**
- `get_lora_parameters_bias()` - Similar pattern including bias access (line 235)

### 2. **Fast LoRA Implementation - Autograd Functions**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

Three core autograd function classes for custom backward passes:

#### **LoRA_MLP (lines 28-174)**
- Handles 3 projection matrices: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
- Parameters: `(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upA, upB, upS, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS, _forward_function, _backward_function, inplace)`
- Math: `G = G + Ag @ Bg` (and similar for U, W)
- Custom backward computes gradients for both base weights and LoRA A/B matrices

#### **LoRA_QKV (lines 224-363)**
- Handles query, key, value projections
- Returns `(Q, K, V)` tuple
- Optimized gradient computation for attention weights

#### **LoRA_W (lines 381-457)**
- Generic single weight matrix handler
- Used for output projection (o_proj)
- Parameters: `(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)`

**Related Functions:**
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` - For SwiGLU MLP blocks (Llama, Mistral, Qwen)
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` - For exact GeGLU (Gemma, Gemma2)
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_approx()` - Approximated GeGLU version
- `apply_lora_qkv()` - QKV projection wrapper
- `apply_lora_o()` - Output projection wrapper

### 3. **Model Classes and Exports**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/loader.py` (line 107)

```python
class FastLanguageModel(FastLlamaModel):
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(
        model_name = "unsloth/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
        # ... many parameters ...
    ):
```

**Key Public Methods:**

1. **`FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`** - Main entry point for loading models with LoRA support
2. Inherits from `FastLlamaModel` which provides all core functionality

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/__init__.py`

```python
from .llama import FastLlamaModel
from .loader import FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel
```

### 4. **LoRA Configuration and Initialization**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/llama.py`

#### **get_peft_model() - lines 2266-2730**

Main function to add LoRA adapters to a base model:

```python
@staticmethod
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,                              # LoRA rank
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,                     # Scaling: alpha/r
    lora_dropout = 0.0,                  # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    bias = "none",                       # Options: "none", "all", "lora_only"
    layers_to_transform = None,
    init_lora_weights = True,            # Options: True, False, "gaussian", "loftq"
    loftq_config = {},                   # LoftQ configuration for 4-bit
    use_rslora = False,                  # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save = None,              # Full-weight modules: ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"]
    **kwargs
):
```

**Key Steps (line 2617):**
```python
lora_config = LoraConfig(**arguments)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

Uses PEFT's LoraConfig and get_peft_model to create the adapter layers.

### 5. **Method Patching for Fast Inference/Training**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/llama.py` (line 1778)

**Class:** `FastLlamaModel`

#### **pre_patch() - lines 1781-1810**
Monkey patches transformer components:
```python
LlamaAttention      .forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaSdpaAttention  .forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaFlashAttention2.forward = LlamaAttention_fast_forward
LlamaDecoderLayer   .forward = LlamaDecoderLayer_fast_forward
LlamaModel          .forward = LlamaModel_fast_forward
LlamaForCausalLM    .forward = CausalLM_fast_forward(...)
PeftModelForCausalLM.forward = PeftModel_fast_forward
```

#### **patch_peft_model() - lines 2732-2948**

Runtime patching of PEFT models to use custom LoRA implementations:

```python
@staticmethod
def patch_peft_model(model, use_gradient_checkpointing="unsloth"):
```

**Patching Logic (lines 2821-2895):**
- Checks if LoRA dropout == 0 and bias == "none" (required for optimization)
- For each transformer layer:
  - **MLP patching** - Replaces forward with `apply_lora_mlp`
  - **QKV patching** - Assigns `layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv`
  - **O projection patching** - Assigns `layer.self_attn.apply_o = apply_lora_o`
- Uses `types.MethodType()` to bind optimized functions as instance methods

**Example (lines 2844-2846):**
```python
mlp_module.forward = types.MethodType(_apply_lora_mlp, mlp_module)
layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv
layer.self_attn.apply_o = apply_lora_o
```

### 6. **Supported Model Architectures**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2751-2762)

```python
if model_type == "llama":     apply_lora_mlp = apply_lora_mlp_swiglu
```

Now let me compile the comprehensive report:

## Unsloth LoRA Implementation - Comprehensive Exploration Report

Based on a thorough exploration of the Unsloth codebase, here is the detailed analysis of how LoRA adapters are implemented:

### 1. **LoRA Weight Storage and Access**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-232)

The LoRA weights are accessed through PEFT's dictionary-based structure:
```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # Access pattern:
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dictionary keyed by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    A = lora_A_linear.weight                # Get .weight from Linear layer
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
```

**Key Points:**
- `lora_A` and `lora_B` are dictionaries keyed by adapter name (e.g., "default")
- Each entry is a Linear layer (from PEFT's Linear adapter)
- Actual weights accessed via `.weight` attribute
- Returns 5-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`

**Related Functions:**
- `get_lora_parameters_bias()` - Similar pattern including bias access (line 235)

### 2. **Fast LoRA Implementation - Autograd Functions**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

Three core autograd function classes for custom backward passes:

#### **LoRA_MLP (lines 28-174)**
- Handles 3 projection matrices: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj
- Parameters: `(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upA, upB, upS, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS, _forward_function, _backward_function, inplace)`
- Math: `G = G + Ag @ Bg` (and similar for U, W)
- Custom backward computes gradients for both base weights and LoRA A/B matrices

#### **LoRA_QKV (lines 224-363)**
- Handles query, key, value projections
- Returns `(Q, K, V)` tuple
- Optimized gradient computation for attention weights

#### **LoRA_W (lines 381-457)**
- Generic single weight matrix handler
- Used for output projection (o_proj)
- Parameters: `(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)`

**Related Functions:**
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` - For SwiGLU MLP blocks (Llama, Mistral, Qwen)
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()` - For exact GeGLU (Gemma, Gemma2)
- `apply_lora_mlp_geglu_approx()` - Approximated GeGLU version
- `apply_lora_qkv()` - QKV projection wrapper
- `apply_lora_o()` - Output projection wrapper

### 3. **Model Classes and Exports**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/loader.py` (line 107)

```python

        # ... many parameters ...
    ):
```

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/llama.py`

```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,                              # LoRA rank
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                      "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,                     # Scaling: alpha/r
    lora_dropout = 0.0,                  # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    bias = "none",                       # Options: "none", "all", "lora_only"
    layers_to_transform = None,
    init_lora_weights = True,            # Options: True, False, "gaussian", "loftq"
    loftq_config = {},                   # LoftQ configuration for 4-bit
    use_rslora = False,                  # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    modules_to_save = None,              # Full-weight modules: ["lm_head", "embed_tokens"]
    **kwargs
):
```

**Key Steps (line 2617):**
```python

model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

Uses PEFT's LoraConfig and get_peft_model to create the adapter layers.

### 5. **Method Patching for Fast Inference/Training**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775305348892-ql2vvv/unsloth/models/llama.py` (line 1778)

#### **patch_peft_model() - lines 2732-2948**

Runtime patching of PEFT models to use custom LoRA implementations:


**Patching Logic (lines 2821-2895):**
- Checks if LoRA dropout == 0 and bias == "none" (required for optimization)

- Uses `types.MethodType()` to bind optimized functions as instance methods

```python
layer.self_attn.apply_qkv = apply_lora_qkv

```

    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",

    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",

)

    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
    # "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass
pass


def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
    """
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
    return X
pass


# INTEL GPU Specific Logic
if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
    @torch.inference_mode
    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
        if quant_state is None: return W
        if type(quant_state) is not list:
            # New quant_state as a class
            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
            shape      = quant_state.shape

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass


@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"
    pass

    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass

    # First check for a token!
    if push_to_hub:
        from huggingface_hub import whoami

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")
